Arrived just before 6, 12th in line. Walked in with Bob Lubbehusen and Ed Warburton -- they saved a seat. She played one song using an all-white right-handed bass with an embedded working neon tube that had been built for Dusty Hill of ZZ Top.
VIP merch: last 2 LPs (most recent autographed), poster, lanyard. Hat signed by Jesse Williams (supporting act, not in Musicbrainz).

Notes / Memories: RHBS signed by Selwyn Birchwood and Regi Oliver; CD & sticker merch

Both artists played solo then invited the other up for part of the set. Amidon: acoustic guitar, banjo, fiddle. JXF: electric and acoustic. ~100 people. Sam mentioned folk artist parents Peter and Mary Alice (1970s). Hat questions from audience; one woman mistook Dan for NPR's Bob Boilen.

Don't Settle Tour. Stage right balcony. 3rd attempt to see Hansard. Prior Apr 2020 Glaspy headline ticket (pandemic cancellation). Glaspy plans new album + headline Fall 2026 tour. Hansard played electric and acoustic; took requests from fans on the rail; covered Gloria (Van Morrison) for encore shouting out Jose Andres. Hat autograph: Margaret Glaspy (supporting act).

Venue Address: 220 N Washington St, Falls Church, VA 22046
Notes / Memories: Impromptu show. First time seeing them since the switch from New York's Finest. Police Cars tribute bill. Did not stay for The Side Cars Band.
Closer To The Bone Tour. Saw Larkin Poe t-shirt and said you have good taste. Invited Charlie Owen from audience to sing Further on Down the Road (cover) before Big Sister's Radio. Signed CD.
RHBS autograph + signed photo from previous show.
Night Two; hometown show. Jennifer, Lary, and Bob in the audience. Collier called Lary by name before playing Icarus. Hat autograph: Laura Chavez.

VIP: only 3 attendees. Sat across from Kanene Pipkin. Played 3 songs incl. Call to War. Talked about Cool Disco Dan shirt, the hat (Kanene claimed to remember it), and being mistaken for Bob Boilen — Zach mentioned hat in that context. Kanene's parents in the audience. Photo taken by Tyler (tour manager / occasional keys). Trio autographed portrait prints from 11/2024 show. Merch: lanyard + signed 2026 tour poster.

Power trio + Deanna Bogart (Tommy Castro's wife) on sax. During drum solo to end set one, Gabe went to Baskin Robbins for a cone and came back eating it. Set two brought up guitarist from the early show. Did not stay for set three. $5 tip jar.

Animals as Leaders: very metal, both guitarists play 8-string guitars, no traditional bassist. SatchVai Band: Joe Satriani & his drummer, Steve Vai and his rhythm guitarist, and a bass player who also did some vocals. Pure guitar virtuosity. Sat with Lary and Guinness record holder Mike Brown.
Misquoted Dan mid-set asking "how's that going?"; said "bless you" after a sneeze.
Fifth in line with Ed. Ran into Jackie and the band leaving the venue to find food; signed poster and t-shirt again, shared a laugh about them turning the wrong way. Discovery Series.
Private Notes: 1 of 2 tickets to budget; Jennifer had the 2nd ticket but was a no-show. Ed left his flask in the theatre
